patch.value:
## Stack trace (Sentry latest event)

culprit: Command._registerCommand (commander)
runPmCli -> registerCoreCommandFamilies -> registerSetupCommands -> Command.command -> Command._registerCommand

## Root cause

src/cli/register-setup.ts:330-332 `registerSetupCommands(program)` does `program.command('init')`. Commander throws when `program` ALREADY has a top-level `init` command at that point. Reproduces in production but not in tests, so the prior `init` registration is workspace-dependent — most likely an installed extension/package whose manifest declares a top-level command named `init`, or the bootstrap executable-alias rewrite (pm-7by2) resolving an alias to `init` before core registration. (Note: register-setup.ts:209 `.command('init')` is the `pm extension/package init` SUBcommand and is a different scope — not the collision.)

## Impact

- Unhandled raw Error reaches Sentry as a high-severity unresolved event.
- Because the title is not a `CommandError:`/standup-export pattern, it is not allowlisted by the release Sentry gate (release-automation-contract.spec.ts) and can fail the gate -> silently block the daily auto-release (pm-nb08 class of failure).

## Proposed fix (separate PR)

- Guard top-level command registration against built-in name collisions: before `program.command('init')`, detect an existing `init` (program.commands) and either skip or throw a normalized PmCliError with an actionable message (which extension declared the colliding command + how to rename/disable).
- Add a regression test that pre-registers a top-level `init` then runs registerSetupCommands and asserts a clean PmCliError (not a raw Commander throw).
- Consider extending the extension command-registration path to reject/parenthesize names that shadow built-in top-level commands.

## Evidence

- Sentry: PM-CLI-1R / 128405024 (unbrained)
- src/cli/register-setup.ts:330-332 (top-level init registration)
- Related: pm-nb08 (auto-release Sentry-gate fragility), pm-yohx (count-agnostic allowlist)
Sentry PM-CLI-1R: 'cannot add command init as already have command init' — top-level init double-registration throws raw Commander error

Production Sentry issue PM-CLI-1R (org unbrained, id 128405024), last seen 2026-06-16, level=error, unresolved. Raw 'Error: cannot add command init as already have command init' — NOT a CommandError, so it is OUTSIDE the Sentry release-gate expected-CLI-error allowlist and can block the daily auto-release (the exact fragility pm-nb08 warns about).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Fixed (PM-CLI-1R). Root cause confirmed via Sentry latest event + stacktrace (runPmCli -> registerCoreCommandFamilies -> registerSetupCommands -> Command.command -> _registerCommand; release pm-cli@2026.6.16, env=production): the root program is a MODULE-LEVEL SINGLETON (main.ts:1740), so any path that enters runPmCli a second time in one process re-runs core command registration against it and Commander throws the raw 'cannot add command init as already have command init' Error. Because it is a raw Error (not a CommandError), it escapes the Sentry release-gate CLI-error allowlist and silently blocks the daily auto-release (the pm-nb08 fragility class; confirmed today's Auto Release run 27673362890 failed with sentry high=1 threshold_ok=false blocking_short_ids=[PM-CLI-1R]). FIX: new ensureIdempotentTopLevelCommandRegistration(program) installed once per program (Symbol flag) at the top of registerCoreCommandFamilies; it wraps program.command so a DUPLICATE top-level name returns a throwaway Command (chained .argument/.option/.action apply harmlessly) while the already-wired original is preserved. Handles all filtered/full selection re-entry combinations (per-command-name, not per-family). First-time registration flows through Commander untouched. Verified: 3x runPmCli in one process no longer crashes; unit test in cli-main-errors.spec.ts covers first-register/duplicate/distinct/guard-reentry branches. NEXT: after merge, resolve Sentry PM-CLI-1R so the release gate clears.
